Class PongWatchdog

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am25.client.webLayer.PongWatchdog
public class PongWatchdog extends Object
Monitors server liveness by tracking the last time any activity was received. If no activity is recorded within the configured threshold, the death callback is invoked. Used by both Socket (ServerListener) and RMI (ClientTUI) clients to apply the same disconnection-detection logic regardless of transport.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    INTERVAL_S
    Interval between consecutive server pings/pongs, in seconds.
    static final int
    MISSED_PONG_THRESHOLD
    Number of consecutive missed pongs before the server is declared dead.
    static final long
    THRESHOLD_MS
    Silence duration in milliseconds after which the server is declared dead.

    Constructor
    Description
    PongWatchdog()
    Creates a new PongWatchdog in the idle state (no scheduler running).

  • Field Details

    • INTERVAL_S

      public static final int INTERVAL_S
      Interval between consecutive server pings/pongs, in seconds.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • MISSED_PONG_THRESHOLD

      public static final int MISSED_PONG_THRESHOLD
      Number of consecutive missed pongs before the server is declared dead.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

      public static final long THRESHOLD_MS
      Silence duration in milliseconds after which the server is declared dead.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • PongWatchdog

      public PongWatchdog()
      Creates a new PongWatchdog in the idle state (no scheduler running). Call start(Runnable) to begin monitoring.

  • Method Details

    • recordActivity

      public void recordActivity()
      Records that the server is still alive. Must be called whenever any message from the server is received (any game message for Socket; pong for RMI).

    • start

      public void start(Runnable onDeath)
      Starts the watchdog monitor. Checks every INTERVAL_S second whether the server has been silent for longer than THRESHOLD_MS; if so, invokes onDeath once and stops.
      Parameters:
      onDeath - callback invoked when the silence threshold is exceeded.

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the watchdog monitor. Safe to call even if start(Runnable) was never called.
